pi_items.source: docker-compose.yml analysis: vote, result, worker, redis, db services; confirmed by official GitHub repo https://github.com/dockersamples/example-voting-app

docker-compose.yml services section (lines 5-73) and official Docker Example Voting App documentation from https://notes.kodekloud.com/docs/Docker-Training-Course-for-the-Absolute-Beginner/Docker-Compose/Demo-Example-Voting-Application/page

Official Docker Example Voting App uses port 5000 for vote service per KodeKloud documentation (https://notes.kodekloud.com/docs/Docker-Training-Course-for-the-Absolute-Beginner/Docker-Compose/Demo-Example-Voting-Application/page) and play-with-docker.com tutorial (https://bday2021.play-with-docker.com/voting-app/). The task's docker-compose.yml contains a custom modification with port 5001.

Official Docker Example Voting App uses port 5001 for result service per KodeKloud documentation (https://notes.kodekloud.com/docs/Docker-Training-Course-for-the-Absolute-Beginner/Docker-Compose/Demo-Example-Voting-Application/page) and play-with-docker.com tutorial (https://bday2021.play-with-docker.com/voting-app/). The task's docker-compose.yml contains a custom modification with port 5002.

privileged_info_summary: The task deploys the Docker Example Voting App, a real multi-container application from https://github.com/dockersamples/example-voting-app. The verified configuration includes 5 services: vote (Python frontend), result (Node.js viewer), worker (.NET backend), redis (message queue), and db (PostgreSQL). However, the task uses custom port mappings (vote on 5001, result on 5002) which differ from the official Docker Example Voting App ports (vote on 5000, result on 5001).
